bins.name: Clear federal-state jurisdiction lines
bins.desc: Enchanted Rock wants stable, predictable, and legally durable rules and joins Vistra in seeking clarity on the dividing line between federal and state regulation of large-load interconnection. It frames predictable rules as necessary for infrastructure investment and business confidence.
quotes: Winning the AI race and bolstering the U.S. economy relies on the ability of federal and state regulators to create stable and predictable rules that encourage infrastructure investment and business confidence.

Vistra's comments align with Enchanted Rock's initial comments seeking further clarification on the dividing line between federal and state regulation of large load interconnection,6 and demonstrate the need for the Commission to promulgate a final rule that is clear, predictable and legally durable.

bins.name: Align NERC standards with reforms
bins.desc: The filer recommends the Commission direct NERC to revise its standards to recognize load flexibility from on-site or co-located generation, and supports harmonizing NERC reliability standards with the reforms under consideration in this proceeding.
quotes: Enchanted Rock previously recommended that the Commission direct the North American
supports efforts to harmonize NERC's reliability standards with the reforms being considered in this ANOPR proceeding.

bins.name: Flexible and bridge load service
bins.desc: Enchanted Rock argues load flexibility lets planners assume lower peak demand, reducing immediate upgrade needs and speeding interconnection. It endorses interruptible and temporary opt-in service as a bridge that lets large loads operate before transmission upgrades complete, and cites SPP's HILLGA and CHILLS as expedited-pathway models.
quotes: Acknowledging load flexibility during the transmission planning and interconnection processes allows transmission planners to assume lower peak demand for the associated load facility, thereby reducing the immediate need for transmission upgrades and expediting interconnection timelines.
interruptible service can serve as a bridge to full service while transmission upgrades are completed.
a temporary flexibility option that large loads can opt into . . . therefore mitigate[ing] some . . . risks and allow[ing] for faster load interconnection.

Efforts such as Southwest Power Pool, Inc. (“SPP”)’s High Impact Large Load Generation Assessment (“HILLGA”) and anticipated Conditional High Impact Large Load Service (“CHILLS”) proposal are examples of proposals that will create expedited pathways for interconnecting large loads.

bins.name: Joint study on net grid impacts
bins.desc: The filer urges the Commission to adopt rules facilitating joint study processes, even provisionally, that evaluate co-located and hybrid facilities such as its LCDG model based on operational behavior and net load impacts, which it says reduces system strain and unlocks grid headroom.
quotes: Enchanted Rock encourages the Commission to adopt rules that will facilitate joint study processes (even if only for a provisional period) and that evaluate co-located and hybrid facilities based on their net impacts to the grid.

a joint study process would allow LCDG and hybrid facilities to be studied based on their operational behavior and net load impacts, which Enchanted Rock believes will result in less strain on the transmission system, unlock available grid headroom, optimize existing transmission infrastructure and more accurately model the flexible capabilities of the system.

bins.name: Joint study of load and generation
bins.desc: Enchanted Rock supports a joint study process that studies co-located load and generation together based on net impacts, arguing it more accurately models flexible system capabilities and optimizes existing infrastructure.
quotes: Enchanted Rock encourages the Commission to adopt rules that will facilitate joint study processes (even if only for a provisional period) and that evaluate co-located and hybrid facilities based on their net impacts to the grid.

a joint study process would allow LCDG and hybrid facilities to be studied based on their operational behavior and net load impacts, which Enchanted Rock believes will result in less strain on the transmission system, unlock available grid headroom, optimize existing transmission infrastructure and more accurately model the flexible capabilities of the system.

bins.name: Co-location reduces transmission strain
bins.desc: The filer promotes co-locating generation with load so planners study the assets as a combined facility, which it says speeds power delivery, expedites study timelines, triggers fewer upgrades, and reduces strain on transmission infrastructure.
quotes: Enchanted Rock encourages the Commission to adopt rules that will facilitate joint study processes (even if only for a provisional period) and that evaluate co-located and hybrid facilities based on their net impacts to the grid.

This observation highlights the value of co-location and its practical application to facilitate speed-to-power, expedite study timelines, protect ratepayer affordability, and reduce strain on transmission infrastructure.
Co-locating generation with load allows system planners to

bins.name: Protect ratepayer affordability
bins.desc: Enchanted Rock ties co-location to ratepayer protection, arguing that studying co-located assets together triggers fewer transmission upgrades and mitigates costs that would otherwise be passed onto ratepayers.
quotes: This observation highlights the value of co-location and its practical application to facilitate speed-to-power, expedite study timelines, protect ratepayer affordability, and reduce strain on transmission infrastructure.

bins.name: Expedited interconnection pathways
bins.desc: The filer cites SPP's HILLGA assessment and anticipated CHILLS proposal as examples of expedited large-load interconnection pathways the Commission should consider as models for other grid operators.
quotes: Efforts such as Southwest Power Pool, Inc. (“SPP”)’s High Impact Large Load Generation Assessment (“HILLGA”) and anticipated Conditional High Impact Large Load Service (“CHILLS”) proposal are examples of proposals that will create expedited pathways for interconnecting large loads.

bins.name: SPP large-load pathways as model
bins.desc: Enchanted Rock points to SPP's High Impact Large Load Generation Assessment and Conditional High Impact Large Load Service proposal as concrete regional examples of expedited large-load interconnection pathways worth adopting elsewhere.
quotes: Efforts such as Southwest Power Pool, Inc. (“SPP”)’s High Impact Large Load Generation Assessment (“HILLGA”) and anticipated Conditional High Impact Large Load Service (“CHILLS”) proposal are examples of proposals that will create expedited pathways for interconnecting large loads.
